feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Process Orchestration
feed.subtitle: SAP Process Orchestration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: PO7.5 Node functions
entry.content:
Hi ,

I am using PO7.5. I am new to node functions and need some help.

Say,I have 3 input fields(ip1, ip2, ip3) and one output field(op1).

I want to achieve below functionality with node functions or UDF. Can anyone guide me on this?

If IP1 or IP2 is initial then OP1 = IP3.

If IP1 is not initial and IP2 is not initial then OP1 = concate(IP1, IP2).

Do i need to create Custome function or with simple node functions is it possible?

Thanks,
Pavan.

entry.title: Please teach me how to Mapping Function [counter]
entry.content:
Hellow, Every one.
My Name is Shuto Suzuki.

Please teach me how to Mapping Function [counter].


When I actually tried setting the mapping definition as above, the output result for ① was 202.
This can be understood by saying,
　``The value of [Increment] was added to the value of [Initial Value] for the number of calls of [counter].''


However, regarding ②, in parts (1) and (2) of the image,
I thought that both would be 420 because "counter" was called twice,
but the result was 210 for both.

Not only the above example ,even other simple mapping examples is okey.
Please teach me about to know how the "counter" function works.

example)
With this kind of mapping, [Initial Value] is this value and [Increment] is this value, so it is calculated like this and the output result is like this.  etc

Best Regards.

entry.title: Problem with REST sender
entry.content:
Hi

I have an "InterfaceDetermination did not yield any actual interface" with my REST sender. I really cannot get it. 

Log says:

But as i understand my setup i has an Integrated  Configuration that matches exactly this:

What am i missing here?

entry.title: Fetch mails from Outlook365 from SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Please could someone let me know how to connect to outlook365 and read mails via SAP PO.

Thanks and Regards,

Sudarshan

entry.title: how to change id of a software component from the SLD - PO PI
entry.content:
Hi,

how do I change the id of a software component from the SLD?

For example

I need to create a new software component, but I need to associate a specific id


Thank you

entry.title: Help with NoClassDefFoundError for com/sap/aii/security/lib/exception/SecurityException in EJB Proj
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am encountering a NoClassDefFoundError for com/sap/aii/security/lib/exception/SecurityException in my EJB project deployed on SAP NetWeaver. Here are the details of my setup and the issue:

Project Setup:

  • I have an EAR project that includes an EJB module and a Dynamic Web Project (DWP).
  • I am using Eclipse for development and SAP PO (Process Orchestration) for deployment.

Issue: When my EJB bean executes the line following line
SAPSecurityResources secRes = SAPSecurityResources.getInstance();
I get the error NoClassDefFoundError: com/sap/aii/security/lib/exception/SecurityException

Here's the full stack trace for the error:

 

javax.ejb.EJBException: nested exception is: java.lang.RuntimeException: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/sap/aii/security/lib/exception/SecurityException
java.lang.RuntimeException: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/sap/aii/security/lib/exception/SecurityException
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.RequestInvocationContext.proceedFinal(RequestInvocationContext.java:99)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.AbstractInvocationContext.proceed(AbstractInvocationContext.java:166)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.Interceptors_StatesTransition.invoke(Interceptors_StatesTransition.java:19)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.AbstractInvocationContext.proceed(AbstractInvocationContext.java:179)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.Interceptors_Resource.invoke(Interceptors_Resource.java:50)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.AbstractInvocationContext.proceed(AbstractInvocationContext.java:179)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.Interceptors_Transaction.doWorkWithAttribute(Interceptors_Transaction.java:37)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.Interceptors_Transaction.invoke(Interceptors_Transaction.java:21)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.AbstractInvocationContext.proceed(AbstractInvocationContext.java:179)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.Interceptors_MethodRetry.invoke(Interceptors_MethodRetry.java:46)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.AbstractInvocationContext.proceed(AbstractInvocationContext.java:179)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.AbstractInvocationContext.proceed(AbstractInvocationContext.java:191)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.Interceptors_StatelessInstanceGetter.invoke(Interceptors_StatelessInstanceGetter.java:23)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.AbstractInvocationContext.proceed(AbstractInvocationContext.java:179)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.Interceptors_SecurityCheck.invoke(Interceptors_SecurityCheck.java:25)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.AbstractInvocationContext.proceed(AbstractInvocationContext.java:179)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.Interceptors_ExceptionTracer.invoke(Interceptors_ExceptionTracer.java:17)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.AbstractInvocationContext.proceed(AbstractInvocationContext.java:179)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.DefaultInvocationChainsManager.startChain(DefaultInvocationChainsManager.java:138)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.DefaultEJBProxyInvocationHandler.invoke(DefaultEJBProxyInvocationHandler.java:172)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.DefaultEJBProxyInvocationHandler.invoke(DefaultEJBProxyInvocationHandler.java:99)
at com.sun.proxy.$Proxy3585.loadCertificate(Unknown Source)
at com.myname.mycompany.myclient.servlet.certificates.CertificateRetrieveServlet.doGet(CertificateRetrieveServlet.java:41)
at javax.servlet.http.HttpServlet.service(HttpServlet.java:734)
at javax.servlet.http.HttpServlet.service(HttpServlet.java:847)
at com.sap.engine.services.servlets_jsp.server.Invokable.invoke(Invokable.java:152)
at com.sap.engine.services.servlets_jsp.server.Invokable.invoke(Invokable.java:38)
at com.sap.engine.services.servlets_jsp.server.HttpHandlerImpl.runServlet(HttpHandlerImpl.java:466)
at com.sap.engine.services.servlets_jsp.server.HttpHandlerImpl.handleRequest(HttpHandlerImpl.java:210)
at com.sap.engine.services.httpserver.server.RequestAnalizer.startServlet(RequestAnalizer.java:438)
at com.sap.engine.services.httpserver.server.RequestAnalizer.startServlet(RequestAnalizer.java:427)
at com.sap.engine.services.servlets_jsp.filters.DSRWebContainerFilter.process(DSRWebContainerFilter.java:38)
at com.sap.engine.services.httpserver.chain.AbstractChain.process(AbstractChain.java:78)
at com.sap.engine.services.servlets_jsp.filters.ServletSelector.process(ServletSelector.java:81)
at com.sap.engine.services.httpserver.chain.AbstractChain.process(AbstractChain.java:78)
at com.sap.engine.services.servlets_jsp.filters.ApplicationSelector.process(ApplicationSelector.java:278)
at com.sap.engine.services.httpserver.chain.AbstractChain.process(AbstractChain.java:78)

at com.sap.engine.services.httpserver.chain.ServerFilter.process(ServerFilter.java:12)
at com.sap.engine.services.httpserver.chain.AbstractChain.process(AbstractChain.java:78)
at com.sap.engine.services.httpserver.server.Processor.chainedRequest(Processor.java:468)
at com.sap.engine.services.httpserver.server.Processor$FCAProcessorThread.process(Processor.java:262)
at com.sap.engine.services.httpserver.server.rcm.RequestProcessorThread.run(RequestProcessorThread.java:56)
at com.sap.engine.core.thread.execution.Executable.run(Executable.java:122)
at com.sap.engine.core.thread.execution.Executable.run(Executable.java:101)
at com.sap.engine.core.thread.execution.CentralExecutor$SingleThread.run(CentralExecutor.java:328)
Caused by: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/sap/aii/security/lib/exception/SecurityException
at com.myname.mycompany.myclient.ejb.certificates.CertificateManagement.loadCertificate(CertificateManagement.java:29)
at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)
at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:62)

at java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:498)
at com.sap.engine.services.ejb3.runtime.impl.RequestInvocationContext.proceedFinal(RequestInvocationContext.java:47)
... 63 more
Caused by: java.lang.ClassNotFoundException: com.sap.aii.security.lib.exception.SecurityException

at com.myname.mycompany.myclient.ejb.certificates.CertificateManagement.loadCertificate(CertificateManagement.java:29)
at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)
at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:62)

... 63 more
Caused by: java.lang.ClassNotFoundException: com.sap.aii.security.lib.exception.SecurityException
------------------------- Loader Info -------------------------
ClassLoader name: [sap.com/myclient2partyLdap-ear-jp-library-loader]
Loader hash code: 75994782
Living status: alive
Direct parent loaders:

[library:tc~je~deployment14_api]
[library:jms_api]
[library:tc~je~jacc~plb]
[library:ejb_api]
[library:tc~je~javax77]
[library:jaxr_api]
Resources:
/usr/sap/PO1/J00/j2ee/cluster/apps/sap.com/myclient2partyLdap-ear-jp/app_libraries_container/lib/com.sap.aii.af.svc.filter.jar
/usr/sap/PO1/J00/j2ee/cluster/apps/sap.com/myclient2partyLdap-ear-jp/app_libraries_container/lib/com.sap.aii.af.svc.facade.jar
/usr/sap/PO1/J00/j2ee/cluster/apps/sap.com/myclient2partyLdap-ear-jp/app_libraries_container/lib/com.sap.aii.af.cpa.svc.filter.jar
/usr/sap/PO1/J00/j2ee/cluster/apps/sap.com/myclient2partyLdap-ear-jp/app_libraries_container/lib/com.sap.security_2.0.0.201214143317.jar
---------------------------------------------------------------
at com.sap.engine.boot.loader.MultiParentClassLoader.loadClass(MultiParentClassLoader.java:280)
at com.sap.engine.boot.loader.MultiParentClassLoader.loadClass(MultiParentClassLoader.java:249)
... 69 more

According to help.sap.com/PI/com/sap/aii/af/service/resource/SAPSecurityResources documentation, only com.sap.aii.af.svc.facade jar file is needed to make an instance of SAPSecurityResources object if you want to access KeyStoreManager and manage certificates, but somehow my code doesn't run.

Steps Taken:
  1. I have added the necessary JAR files to the EAR project's lib directory.
  2. I have ensured these JAR files are included in the EAR deployment assembly.

I have also added these JAR files to the EJB and Dynamic Web Projects' build paths in Eclipse.

Any help would be greatly appreciated. 


other Questions:asdad

  1. Is it sufficient to add the required JARs only to the EAR lib directory, or do they need to be explicitly referenced in the EJB and DWP projects' deployment assembly?

P.S I have accessed keystore views in java mappings using the exact same api but in the setup mentioned above I get the NoClassDefFoundError.

Thanks
Syed Ijlal Ayub

entry.title: converting XML fields into single json string
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 

My scenario : proxy <--> PI <---> rest         mode of communication : Synchronous

i have a requirement, i am getting 3 xml fields as shown in the below  in the input payload need to convert them as a single json string. could you please help on this to write an XSLT code. i am new to this xslt code.

input fields : 

client_id

client_secret

grant_type

 

Thanks in advance.

 

Best Regards,

Nagaraju

entry.title: SAP NWBPM calling parameterized mapping from ESR
entry.content:
Hi folks,

I have imported a parameterized interface mapping from ESR into NWBPM (7.5 SP25) and I am calling it inside an automated activity. However I can't find a way to pass a value to the parameter. On the Input mapping tab I can fill the input structure fine but no option for the parameter. Please point me in the right direction!

Thanks

entry.title: IDOC structure not displaying in Xpath condition editor
entry.content:
IDOC structure is not displaying in Xpath condition editor for only 1 Idoc and only in production environment when we want to add a condition on the receiver interface tab. This was working before because there were conditions  set on the receiver itself. However if we want to add a new condition there it also does not show the Idoc structure in the xpath editor on the receiver tab.

The  Idoc is visible in the Business System in the Sender tab and the SWCV is visible in the tab Other Attributes.

The Idoc version is the same as in development and quality where this problem does not occur. SLD cache has been refreshed many times, without any effect. CPA cache only delta because this is in production.

I have read the different questions and answers on this forum for the same problem. Unfortunately those solutions did not help

Solved: IDOC structure not displaying in Xpath condition e... - SAP Community

Solved: IDoc Message Structure Missing from Condition Edit... - SAP Community

Solved: Idoc structure not displying in XPATH at Receiver ... - SAP Community

I have two questions:

1. Does anyone have another idea what this could be and how to fix it ? Would a restart solve it ?

2.  If we add the condition for the receiver interface manually will it work ? I see contradictionary statements about this manually adding conditions.

Any suggestion is welcome. It is a change for production so it is kind of urgent. Thanks.

Regards,

Peter

entry.title: message in sxi_monitor in ECC status log version but in PI is sucessfully
entry.content:
Dear expert

I am facing a strange issue in my synchronous scenario: ECC(proxy)->PI->BPM(soap). 

 Some of the messages for this scenario are stuck in sxi_monitor with status "Log Version" (grey circle sign). Inside sxi_monitor traces, there is no response. but I can see everything has processed successfully in PI and already responded to the message to ECC.

I suspected it was because the parameter timeout in PI was too short. So I set a timeout of 5 minutes in channel.But this situation still happens. From the PI log, the message is responded  within 1 minute.

Please help how should i proceed. I have already spent half a day searching over SDN and other sites....

Thanks very much.

 

##SAP Process Orchestration #SAP S

entry.title: Manuals SAP PI PO Cloud Integration Data Services
entry.content:
hi,

where can I find manuals to get started on sap PI / PO, sap cloud integration and sap data services?


I would need manuals such as which explains what it is: Enterprise Services Repository


Thank you

entry.title: SyncAsyncBridge not getting the response in PRD ... DEV is working
entry.content:
Hi 

I have a syncAsync bridge scenario set up in SAP PO. That's working fine in DEV. Now I moved to PRD and there the bridge is not working.

The WaitingResponseBean is not receiving any messages with the correlationId that it is waiting for.

Is there a way to monitor this (besides the audit log in monitoring?>? 

Do specific services need to run?

 

Ron

entry.title: PI/PO To CPI Migration
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We are planning for PI/PO to CPI migration. We also have our S/4 Rise migration planned. So I would like to know if we should plan the PI/PO to CPI migration after the S/4 Rise migration or if we can start the PI/PO to CPI migration before that. What could be the pros and cons of doing the PI/PO to CPI migration before the S/4 Rise migration?

 

SAP Integration Suite Cloud Integration SAP Process Integration SAP Process Orchestration RISE with SAP 


Thanks and Regards
Sachin Kumar Singh

entry.title: Rest Sender - No channel found to handle "GET" request
entry.content:
Hi

Have an issue with rest sender and passing parameter: 

This setup works okay: 

When calling from Postman RESTAdapter/api/TOCtechloc/TechLocationInformation/id/IF12345
ID gets passed trough allright. 
But i would like to enable "normal" rest parameter call:
RESTAdapter/api/TOCtechloc/TechLocationInformation?id=IF12345

Then i get error:

No channel found to handle "GET" request to "/api/TOCtechloc/TechLocationInformation"

Any ideas of what i am doing wrong?

entry.title: Errors in SAP Backend SXMB_MONI
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have outbound interfaces from SAP ECC to partner systems via SAP PO and we are from SAP PO side.

Could you please help us to identify the root cause of the issue and to avoid this issue in future?

Please find below screenshot that shows the error in SXMB_MONI and is there something that can be done on SAP PO side. If not who can help us to resolve the issue and also ensure that these messages in SXMB_MONI wont occur again in future? Thank you!

Regards,

Ramu.

entry.title: SXI_MONITOR: filter in SXMS_LMS_CONF - look up all not empty values
entry.content:
Hi.

I'm investigating possibilities look up all messages via SXI_MONITOR where certain node has a value

I've tried to create a filter in SXMS_LMS_CONF with

[string-length(text()) > 0]

in the end of my XPath statement, but it cause error during test run.

If there any option for this?

ECC6, NW750

entry.title: Migration of BPM application to cloud
entry.content: We are migrating from S/4 Hana on prem 1909 to S/4 Hana on cloud under Rise with SAP offering. We have developed a number of applications developed on BPM. How can we migrate to cloud without redeveloping the extensive list of BPMs .

entry.title: Can we use SAP PI/PO system and SAP Solution Manager in SAP RISE
entry.content:
Background: Planning a migration to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (Rise) and concerned about the future of certain tools that we are currently using:

Questions 1) Currently have SAP Solution Manager 7.2 in SQL Server 2012 on premise and  would like to know if it is worth planning some type of SQL Server upgrade for this project or is there any other tool that will replace the functionality of the Solution Manager in SAP Rise ?

Question 2) Having 2 PI servers (Process Integrator 7.5) in SQL Server 2012 on premise that act as middleware between the ERP and (SAP SuccessFactors or SAP Concur), and want to know the same way in this conversion process / migration to SAP Rise, how would this component function in the new Rise architecture, will it be replaced by which tool?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Issue while connecting single S4 SAP system with 2 different PI/PO systems
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a requirement to process BP master data (example :BusinessPartnerSUITEBulkReplicateRequest_Out) from S4 system to multiple PI/PO systems.

we have configured RFC destinations, sxmb_admin parameters as per first solution provided in blog  https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/proxy-for-multiple-esrs/ba-p/13332250

IE Config: (Proxy settings at S4)

Category

parameters

subparameter

Current value

Default value

runtime

Is_url

 

dest://PI1_ADVANCE_ADAPTER_ENGINE

 

runtime

Is_url

PISYSTEM2

dest://PI2_ADVANCE_ADAPTER_ENGINE

 

The S4 system will trigger the standard service 'BusinessPartnerSUITEBulkReplicateRequest_Out'. and in both PI/PO systems, we are using standard XICONTENT and configured ICO as passthrough. (no mapping and no ESR). 

The issue we're facing is that when we trigger data from the ECC system, it always processes to PI system 1 only. i.e. default destination configured at sxmb_admin -->IE config. eventhough we configured runtime parameter IS_URL for second PI/PO system.

So the question is:  when S4 trigger BP data, given that the name space will be same, how does S4 differentiate to which PI system it should send data to?  the IS_URL configured for both PI /PO system 1 and 2 and for system2 the subparameter config done at sender/receiver id however it still shows same namespace.

is there anything we need to change at proxy code level? for the abap class 'CL_XMS_PLSRV_IE_ADAPTER-ENTER'? or any changes at PI/PO ICO side? please advise.

entry.title: Fetch the data between dates using select query in JDBC receiver
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

My requirement is REST to JDBC synchronous scenario using select query statement I need to pull data from database and send response back to REST API.

I have gone through the below article 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-pi-proxy-to-jdbc-scenario/ba-p/13326556

I am able to receive the response using the select statement in receiver JDBC. However, I need to select the data in certain period like 10 minutes using specific date field. 

Like if I give 02-07-2024 10:00:00 as input date for Select SQL statement then it should fetch data for next 10 minutes using the same date field which is 

Data between 02-07-2024 10:00:00 to 02-07-2024 10:10:00 

Please help.
